name: Codex
description: Codex app-server harness and native session catalog.

sessionCatalog.label: Native Session Discovery
sessionCatalog.help: Controls whether Codex sessions from this Gateway and paired nodes appear in the sidebar. Disabling also removes this host's paired-node catalog commands without disabling the Codex provider or harness. Node-list failures usually indicate a Gateway pairing-store or node-registry problem; verify Settings > Devices or run openclaw nodes list. Changes require a Gateway restart.

sessionCatalog.enabled.label: Discover Codex Sessions
sessionCatalog.enabled.help: List native Codex sessions in the sidebar from this Gateway and eligible paired nodes.

codexDynamicToolsLoading.label: Dynamic Tools Loading
codexDynamicToolsLoading.help: Use searchable to defer OpenClaw dynamic tools behind Codex tool search, or direct to expose them in the initial context.

codexDynamicToolsExclude.label: Dynamic Tool Excludes
codexDynamicToolsExclude.help: Additional OpenClaw dynamic tool names to omit from Codex app-server turns.

discovery.label: Model Discovery
discovery.help: Plugin-owned controls for discovering Codex app-server models.

discovery.enabled.label: Enable Discovery
discovery.enabled.help: When false, OpenClaw keeps the Codex harness available but uses the bundled fallback model list.

discovery.timeoutMs.label: Discovery Timeout
discovery.timeoutMs.help: Maximum time to wait for Codex app-server model discovery before falling back to the bundled model list.

computerUse.label: Computer Use
computerUse.help: Controls Codex app-server setup for the Computer Use plugin.

computerUse.enabled.label: Enable Computer Use
computerUse.enabled.help: When true, Codex-mode turns require the configured Computer Use MCP server to be available.

computerUse.autoInstall.label: Auto Install
computerUse.autoInstall.help: Install the configured Computer Use plugin when Codex-mode turns start.

computerUse.marketplaceDiscoveryTimeoutM.label: Marketplace Discovery Timeout
computerUse.marketplaceDiscoveryTimeoutM.help: Maximum time to wait for Codex app-server to finish loading marketplaces during Computer Use install.

computerUse.liveTestTimeoutMs.label: Live Test Timeout
computerUse.liveTestTimeoutMs.help: Maximum time for the Computer Use list_apps readiness probe before status and startup treat the live test as failed.

computerUse.toolCallTimeoutMs.label: Tool Call Timeout
computerUse.toolCallTimeoutMs.help: Maximum expected time for real Computer Use tool calls such as list_apps before OpenClaw treats the child runtime as stale.

computerUse.healthCheckEnabled.label: Periodic Health Checks
computerUse.healthCheckEnabled.help: When true, run periodic Computer Use live probes on the configured cadence.

computerUse.healthCheckIntervalMinutes.label: Health Check Interval
computerUse.healthCheckIntervalMinutes.help: Cadence for periodic Computer Use health checks when health checks are enabled.

computerUse.pluginCacheMode.label: Plugin Cache Mode
computerUse.pluginCacheMode.help: The default independent mode leaves each Codex home unmanaged. Choose shared to opt in to a refreshed Codex-discoverable cache copy.

computerUse.strictReadiness.label: Strict Readiness
computerUse.strictReadiness.help: When true, a failed Computer Use live probe stops Codex-mode startup. The default false value preserves existing enabled setups by continuing with a warning.

computerUse.autoRepair.label: Auto Repair
computerUse.autoRepair.help: When true, failed Computer Use live tests repair stale scoped Computer Use MCP children before retrying once.

computerUse.marketplaceSource.label: Marketplace Source
computerUse.marketplaceSource.help: Optional Codex marketplace source to add before installing Computer Use.

computerUse.marketplacePath.label: Marketplace Path
computerUse.marketplacePath.help: Optional local Codex marketplace file path containing the Computer Use plugin.

computerUse.marketplaceName.label: Marketplace Name
computerUse.marketplaceName.help: Optional registered Codex marketplace name containing the Computer Use plugin.

computerUse.pluginName.label: Plugin Name
computerUse.pluginName.help: Codex marketplace plugin name for Computer Use.

computerUse.mcpServerName.label: MCP Server Name
computerUse.mcpServerName.help: MCP server name exposed by the Computer Use plugin.

codexPlugins.label: Native Codex Plugins
codexPlugins.help: Controls native Codex plugin availability for Codex harness turns.

codexPlugins.enabled.label: Enable Native Plugins
codexPlugins.enabled.help: Expose explicit Codex plugin entries to Codex harness turns.

codexPlugins.allow_all_plugins.label: Allow All Connected Apps
codexPlugins.allow_all_plugins.help: Expose every currently accessible app connected to the authenticated Codex account when a new Codex thread starts.

codexPlugins.allow_destructive_actions.label: Allow Destructive Plugin Actions
codexPlugins.allow_destructive_actions.help: Default policy for plugin app write or destructive action elicitations. Use true to accept safe schemas without prompting, false to decline, auto to ask through plugin approvals when Codex requires approval, or ask to prompt for every write/destructive action without durable approval.

codexPlugins.plugins.label: Plugin Entries
codexPlugins.plugins.help: Explicit plugin entries. The wildcard key * is not supported.

supervision.label: Codex Supervision
supervision.help: Configure the separate user-home App Server connection used by the native session catalog and optional supervision tools.

supervision.enabled.label: Enable Codex Supervision
supervision.enabled.help: Enable agent-facing Codex supervision tools. The operator session catalog remains available whenever the plugin is active.

supervision.endpoints.label: Legacy Supervisor Endpoints
supervision.endpoints.help: Advanced compatibility endpoints for migrated Codex Supervisor agent tools.

supervision.allowRawTranscripts.label: Allow Raw Transcript Tools
supervision.allowRawTranscripts.help: Allow supervision agent tools, including turn-inclusive codex_threads reads, to expose full transcripts and transcript previews.

supervision.allowWriteControls.label: Allow Write Control Tools
supervision.allowWriteControls.help: Allow supervision agent tools to control turns and let codex_threads fork, rename, archive, or unarchive native threads.

appServer.label: App Server
appServer.help: Runtime controls for connecting to Codex app-server.

appServer.mode.label: Execution Mode
appServer.mode.help: Legacy Codex app-server preset. Prefer tools.exec.mode=auto for normalized Guardian-reviewed approvals.

appServer.transport.label: Transport
appServer.transport.help: Use stdio to spawn Codex locally or unix for the shared local control socket. Remote websocket transport is experimental and unsupported by Codex.

appServer.homeScope.label: Codex Home Scope
appServer.homeScope.help: Use agent for isolated Codex state, or user to share native Codex threads, config, and authentication with Codex Desktop and the CLI.

appServer.command.label: Command
appServer.command.help: Executable used for stdio transport. Leave unset to use OpenClaw's managed Codex binary.

appServer.args.label: Arguments
appServer.args.help: Arguments used for stdio transport. Defaults to app-server --listen stdio://.

appServer.url.label: WebSocket URL
appServer.url.help: Codex app-server WebSocket URL when transport is websocket.

appServer.authToken.label: Auth Token
appServer.authToken.help: Bearer token sent to the WebSocket app-server.

appServer.headers.label: Headers
appServer.headers.help: Additional headers sent to the WebSocket app-server.

appServer.clearEnv.label: Clear Environment
appServer.clearEnv.help: Environment variable names removed from the spawned stdio app-server process after overrides are applied.

appServer.remoteWorkspaceRoot.label: Remote Workspace Root
appServer.remoteWorkspaceRoot.help: Remote Codex app-server workspace root used to project OpenClaw cwd suffixes before starting Codex threads.

appServer.codeModeOnly.label: Code Mode Only
appServer.codeModeOnly.help: Expose Codex's code-mode-only tool surface. OpenClaw dynamic tools remain available through Codex nested tool calls.

appServer.loopDetectionPreToolUseRelay.label: Loop Detection Pre-tool Relay
appServer.loopDetectionPreToolUseRelay.help: Install the Codex PreToolUse subprocess used only for OpenClaw loop detection and its no-policy marker. Disable to reduce per-tool process fan-out; before-tool and trusted policy relays remain enforced.

appServer.requestTimeoutMs.label: Request Timeout
appServer.requestTimeoutMs.help: Maximum time to wait for Codex app-server control-plane requests.

appServer.turnCompletionIdleTimeoutMs.label: Turn Completion Idle Timeout
appServer.turnCompletionIdleTimeoutMs.help: Maximum quiet time after Codex accepts a turn or after a turn-scoped app-server request before OpenClaw interrupts the turn while waiting for turn/completed.

appServer.turnAssistantCompletionIdleTim.label: Assistant Completion Idle Timeout
appServer.turnAssistantCompletionIdleTim.help: Short quiet-time guard after Codex reports a completed assistant message before OpenClaw releases the turn.

appServer.postToolRawAssistantCompletion.label: Post-Tool Continuation Idle Timeout
appServer.postToolRawAssistantCompletion.help: Completion-idle and progress guard after a tool handoff, native tool completion, or post-tool raw assistant progress while waiting for turn/completed. Defaults to 300000 ms when unset.

appServer.approvalPolicy.label: Approval Policy
appServer.approvalPolicy.help: Codex native approval policy sent to thread start, resume, and turns.

appServer.sandbox.label: Sandbox
appServer.sandbox.help: Codex native sandbox mode sent to thread start and resume.

appServer.approvalsReviewer.label: Approvals Reviewer
appServer.approvalsReviewer.help: Use user approvals or Codex auto_review for native app-server approvals. guardian_subagent remains accepted for compatibility.

appServer.serviceTier.label: Service Tier
appServer.serviceTier.help: Optional Codex app-server service tier. Use priority, flex, or null. Legacy fast is accepted as priority.

appServer.networkProxy.label: Network Proxy
appServer.networkProxy.help: Enable Codex permissions-profile networking for app-server commands.

appServer.networkProxy.enabled.label: Network Proxy Enabled
appServer.networkProxy.enabled.help: When enabled, OpenClaw defines a Codex permissions profile and selects it with default_permissions instead of sandbox fields.

appServer.networkProxy.profileName.label: Network Proxy Profile
appServer.networkProxy.profileName.help: Optional stable Codex permissions profile name. Leave unset to use a generated openclaw-network fingerprint name.

appServer.networkProxy.baseProfile.label: Network Proxy Base
appServer.networkProxy.baseProfile.help: Filesystem access used by the generated profile. Defaults to read-only for read-only sandboxes and workspace otherwise.

appServer.networkProxy.domains.label: Network Domains
appServer.networkProxy.domains.help: Domain allow and deny rules for Codex sandboxed networking.

appServer.networkProxy.unixSockets.label: Unix Sockets
appServer.networkProxy.unixSockets.help: Unix socket allow and none rules for Codex sandboxed networking.

appServer.networkProxy.proxyUrl.label: HTTP Proxy URL
appServer.networkProxy.proxyUrl.help: HTTP listener URL used by Codex sandboxed networking.

appServer.networkProxy.socksUrl.label: SOCKS Proxy URL
appServer.networkProxy.socksUrl.help: SOCKS listener URL used by Codex sandboxed networking.

appServer.networkProxy.enableSocks5.label: Enable SOCKS5
appServer.networkProxy.enableSocks5.help: Expose SOCKS5 support for the generated Codex permissions profile.

appServer.networkProxy.enableSocks5Udp.label: Enable SOCKS5 UDP
appServer.networkProxy.enableSocks5Udp.help: Allow UDP over the SOCKS5 listener when SOCKS5 is enabled.

appServer.networkProxy.allowUpstreamProx.label: Allow Upstream Proxy
appServer.networkProxy.allowUpstreamProx.help: Allow Codex sandboxed networking to chain through inherited HTTP(S)_PROXY or ALL_PROXY settings.

appServer.networkProxy.allowLocalBinding.label: Allow Local Binding
appServer.networkProxy.allowLocalBinding.help: Permit broader local and private-network access through Codex sandboxed networking.

appServer.networkProxy.mode.label: Network Mode
appServer.networkProxy.mode.help: Codex sandboxed networking mode for subprocess traffic.

appServer.networkProxy.dangerouslyAllowN.label: Allow Non-Loopback Proxy
appServer.networkProxy.dangerouslyAllowN.help: Permit non-loopback bind addresses for Codex sandboxed networking listeners.

appServer.networkProxy.dangerouslyAllowA.label: Allow All Unix Sockets
appServer.networkProxy.dangerouslyAllowA.help: Bypass Codex's Unix socket allowlist for tightly controlled environments.

appServer.defaultWorkspaceDir.label: Default Workspace
appServer.defaultWorkspaceDir.help: Workspace used by /codex bind when --cwd is omitted.

appServer.experimental.sandboxExecServer.label: Sandbox Exec Server
appServer.experimental.sandboxExecServer.help: Route native Codex execution through an OpenClaw sandbox-backed exec-server when OpenClaw sandboxing is active.
